etymology.description: phonetic 敄 + semantic 力 ('strength') -> affairs; duties; to attend to
etymology.interpretation: Phonetic 敄 lends the sound, while the semantic component 力 (lì, 'strength') suggests the exertion of effort needed to manage 'affairs' or 'duties.' Essentially, it embodies attending to affairs with effort or strength, linking to the sense of 'to attend to.'
etymology.simplification: 務 (wù) was simplified by removing the component 矛 (máo, 'spear') while keeping the 力 component combined with a simplified form of the phonetic component.

examples.chinese: 小王总是认真负责地处理各项业务(小王總是認真負責地處理各項業務)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo Wáng zǒng shì rènzhēn fùzé de chǔlǐ gè xiàng yèwù.
examples.english: Xiao Wang always handles various tasks diligently and responsibly.

examples.chinese: 他应对紧急事务时(他應對緊急事務時)，表现得很镇定(表現得很鎮定)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yìngduì jǐnjí shìwù shí, biǎoxiàn de hěn zhèndìng.
examples.english: He remained very calm when dealing with urgent matters.

examples.chinese: 务必按时完成这个项目(務必按時完成這個項目)。
examples.english: Make sure to complete this project on time.
